verdict_reasoning: ABCB9/TAPL is a multi-pass transmembrane protein that resides on the limiting membrane of lysosomes, where it transports peptides from the cytosol into the lysosomal lumen. Its lysosomal targeting is mediated by its N-terminal transmembrane domain (TMD0), and full-length ABCB9 traffics exclusively to lysosomes via ER→Golgi→endosome→lysosome. As a lysosomal TM protein, it can transiently appear at the plasma membrane during lysosomal exocytosis, the well-documented mechanism by which lysosomal membrane fusion with the PM exposes lysosomal membrane constituents on the cell surface. No stable, constitutive plasma membrane pool is documented for the full-length protein (truncated core domain without TMD0 mislocalizes to PM, but this is an artifact). No evidence of cell-surface-directed therapeutics, ectodomain shedding, secreted form, or stable extracellular-face anchoring. Verdict is contextual/lysosomal_exocytosis because the protein is a bona fide lysosomal TM protein subject to the universal lysosomal exocytosis mechanism.
predicted_key_uncertainty: No gene-specific experimental documentation of ABCB9 appearing at the plasma membrane during lysosomal exocytosis; call rests on the class-level mechanism applicable to all lysosomal TM proteins.
